name: Coverage.IHSFusion
functional_semantic: Fuses an RGB coverage and a panchromatic coverage using the IHS fusion method and returns a fused coverage.
details_description: Selects RGB bands (B2, B3, B4) and the panchromatic band (B8) from the same scene, then fuses the RGB and panchromatic coverages with this operator. The fused result is intended for visualization with enhanced spatial detail.

examples.title: Example: Coverage.IHSFusion
examples.description: Select RGB and panchromatic bands from an image, apply IHS fusion, and visualize the fused result.
examples.code:
import oge
oge.initialize()
service = oge.Service.initialize()

lc08 = service.getCoverage(coverageID="LC81220392015275LGN00", productID="LC08_L1T")
lc08_rgb = service.getProcess("Coverage.selectBands").execute(lc08, ["B2", "B3", "B4"])
lc08_pan = service.getProcess("Coverage.selectBands").execute(lc08, ["B8"])

fusion = service.getProcess("Coverage.IHSFusion").execute(
  lc08_rgb,  # coverage1
  lc08_pan  # coverage2
)

# Visualization
vis_params = {"palette": ["#808080", "#949494", "#a9a9a9", "#bdbebd", "#d3d3d3","#e9e9e9"]}
fusion.styles(vis_params).getMap("fusion")
oge.mapclient.centerMap(114.30, 30.608, 10)

examples.title: Example: Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI) calculation
examples.description: Load a Landsat 8 image, select B4 and B5, compute NDVI, and visualize the result.
examples.code:
import oge
# Initialize
oge.initialize()
service = oge.Service()
# Read Landsat 8 image
lc08 = service.getCoverage(coverageID="LC81220392015275LGN00", productID="LC08_L1T")
# Convert data to floating point to ensure correct NDVI calculation
lc08_d = service.getProcess("Coverage.toFloat").execute(lc08)
b4 = service.getProcess("Coverage.selectBands").execute(lc08, ["B4"])
b5 = service.getProcess("Coverage.selectBands").execute(lc08, ["B5"])
ndvi = service.getProcess("Coverage.NDVI").execute(b5, b4)
vis_params = { "min": -1, "max": 1, "palette": ["gold", "yellow", "green", "lightblue"]}
# Output result layer
ndvi.styles(vis_params).getMap("ndvi")
# Set map center
oge.mapclient.centerMap(114.30, 30.608, 9)

name: Coverage.binarization
functional_semantic: Converts grayscale or continuous raster values to binary classes using one or more thresholds.
details_description: Binarizes the input Coverage according to a threshold. It is commonly used to convert continuous numerical results, such as NDVI, into two-class outputs such as 0/1 or black/white for subsequent analysis or display.

examples.title: Example: binarization
examples.description: Compute NDVI, scale it, apply threshold binarization with Coverage.binarization, then run focalMedian filtering and render the result.
examples.code:
import oge

# Initialize
oge.initialize()
service = oge.Service()

# Read data
lc08 = service.getCoverage(coverageID="LC81220392015275LGN00", productID="LC08_L1T")
lc08 = service.getProcess("Coverage.toFloat").execute(lc08)
# ⽤data
ndvi = service.getProcess("Coverage.normalizedDifference").execute(lc08, ["B5", "B4"])
ndvi = service.getProcess("Coverage.multiplyNum").execute(ndvi, 100.0)
# binarization
Ndvi_binarization = service.getProcess("Coverage.binarization").execute(ndvi, 3)
# median filtering
Ndvi_binarization_Median = service.getProcess("Coverage.focalMedian").execute(Ndvi_binarization,"square",1)
# Set visualization style
vis_params = { "palette": ["black","white"]}
# Output result
Ndvi_binarization_Median.styles(vis_params).getMap("NDVI")
# Set the frontend map center
oge.mapclient.centerMap(114.28, 30.57, 9)

name: Coverage.calNPP
functional_semantic: Estimates Net Primary Productivity from LSWI, NDVI, and auxiliary parameter rasters.
details_description: Takes LSWI, NDVI, and parameter rasters used for NPP calculation, such as temperature and solar-radiation meteorological drivers, and outputs a Net Primary Productivity Coverage. The operator is suitable for vegetation productivity estimation.

examples.title: Example: Net Primary Productivity (NPP) calculation
examples.description: Compute LSWI and NDVI first, then combine them with auxiliary parameter rasters to estimate NPP and display the result.
examples.code:
import oge

oge.initialize()
service = oge.Service.initialize()

lc08 = service.getCoverage(coverageID="LC81240402016340LGN00", productID="LC08_L1T")

auxiliaryData = service.getCoverage(coverageID="OGE_Case_Data/auxiliaryDataSet.tif")

imageLSWI = service.getProcess("Coverage.normalizedDifference").execute(lc08, ["B5", "B6"])

imageNDVI = service.getProcess("Coverage.normalizedDifference").execute(lc08, ["B5", "B4"])

imageNPP = service.getProcess("Coverage.calNPP").execute(imageLSWI, imageNDVI, auxiliaryData)

vis_params = {'min': 0, 'max': 255,"palette": ["#99d8c9","#66c2a4", "#41ae76", "#238b45","#005824"]}
oge.mapclient.centerMap(113.2, 28.5, 8)
imageNPP.styles(vis_params).getMap()

name: Coverage.entropy

The example computes moving-window entropy with radius=2 for a single image band and visualizes it.

name: Coverage.gbtRegressionModel
functional_semantic: Trains a Gradient-Boosted Trees regression model from feature and label coverages.
details_description: Selects multiple image bands as featuresCoverage and a single band as labelCoverage, then passes parameters such as tree depth, iteration count, and learning rate to train and export a GBT regression model.

inputs.description: Minimum number of instances required to split a node.

inputs.description: Minimum fraction of the total weight needed to split a node.

examples.title: Example: Coverage.gbtRegressionModel
examples.description: Use multiband imagery as features and a single-band image as labels, train a GBT regression model, and export the model file.
examples.code:
import oge
# Initialize
oge.initialize()
service = oge.Service.initialize()

# obtain and image
lc08 = service.getCoverage(coverageID="LC81220392015275LGN00", productID="LC08_L1T")
lc08_select = service.getProcess("Coverage.selectBands").execute(lc08, ["B1", "B2", "B3", "B4"])
lc08_label = service.getProcess("Coverage.selectBands").execute(lc08, ["B5"])

# GBT 
model = service.getProcess("Coverage.gbtRegressionModel").execute(
  lc08_select,   # featuresCoverage
  lc08_label,    # labelCoverage
  10,        # checkpointInterval
  "auto",      # featureSubsetStrategy
  "variance",    # impurity
  "squared",     # lossType
  32,        # maxBins
  5,         # maxDepth
  10,        # maxIter
  0.0,        # minInfoGain
  1,         # minInstancesPerNode
  0.0,        # minWeightFractionPerNode
  0,         # seed
  0.1,        # stepSize
  1.0        # subsamplingRate
)

# 
model.export("model")

name: Coverage.geoDetector
functional_semantic: Evaluates the explanatory power of stratified factor rasters for the spatial heterogeneity of a dependent raster variable.
details_description: Takes a dependent-variable raster, factor names, stratified factor rasters, the normal value range of the dependent variable, and invalid-value settings, and outputs Geodetector statistics as a string. It is commonly used to analyze the explanatory power of terrain or environmental factors for spatial differences in a target variable.

inputs.name: norExtent_sta
inputs.description: Start value of the valid normal range.

inputs.name: norExtent_end
inputs.description: End value of the valid normal range.

examples.title: Example: Geodetector analysis
examples.description: Construct a dependent-variable raster and stratified factor rasters, then run Geodetector to output explanatory-power statistics.
examples.code:
import oge

oge.initialize()
service = oge.Service()

landsat = service.getCoverage(
  coverageID="LC81270372016169LGN00",
  productID="LC08_L1T"
)

dem = service.getCoverage(
  coverageID="ASTGTM_N33E108",
  productID="ASTER_GDEM_DEM30"
)

NaN_value = 0

landsat_double = service.getProcess(
  "Coverage.toDouble"
).execute(landsat)

ndvi = service.getProcess(
  "Coverage.normalizedDifference"
).execute(landsat_double, ["B5", "B4"])

ndvi_scaled = service.getProcess(
  "Coverage.multiplyNum"
).execute(ndvi, 100.0)

ndvi_clip = service.getProcess(
  "Coverage.rasterUnion"
).execute(dem, ndvi_scaled)

dem_clip = service.getProcess(
  "Coverage.rasterUnion"
).execute(ndvi_clip, dem)

slope = service.getProcess(
  "Coverage.terrSlope"
).execute(dem_clip, 1, 9.5e-06)

aspect = service.getProcess(
  "Coverage.terrAspect"
).execute(dem_clip, 1)

focal_max = service.getProcess(
  "Coverage.focalMax"
).execute(dem_clip, "circle", 5)

focal_min = service.getProcess(
  "Coverage.focalMin"
).execute(dem_clip, "circle", 5)

relief = service.getProcess(
  "Coverage.subtract"
).execute(focal_max, focal_min)

dem_rules = [
  (0.0, 1000.0, 1.0),
  (1000.0, 1500.0, 2.0),
  (1500.0, 2000.0, 3.0),
  (2000.0, 2500.0, 4.0),
  (2500.0, 5000.0, 5.0)
]
dem_class = service.getProcess(
  "Coverage.reclass"
).execute(dem_clip, dem_rules, NaN_value)

slope_rules = [
  (0.0, 5.0, 1.0),
  (5.0, 15.0, 2.0),
  (15.0, 25.0, 3.0),
  (25.0, 35.0, 4.0),
  (35.0, 90.0, 5.0)
]
slope_class = service.getProcess(
  "Coverage.reclass"
).execute(slope, slope_rules, NaN_value)

aspect_rules = [
  (0.0, 45.0, 1.0),
  (45.0, 135.0, 2.0),
  (135.0, 225.0, 3.0),
  (225.0, 315.0, 4.0),
  (315.0, 361.0, 1.0)
]
aspect_class = service.getProcess(
  "Coverage.reclass"
).execute(aspect, aspect_rules, NaN_value)

relief_rules = [
  (0.0, 50.0, 1.0),
  (50.0, 100.0, 2.0),
  (100.0, 200.0, 3.0),
  (200.0, 350.0, 4.0),
  (350.0, 5000.0, 5.0)
]
relief_class = service.getProcess(
  "Coverage.reclass"
).execute(relief, relief_rules, NaN_value)

ndvi_rules = [
  (-100.0, 0.0, 1.0),
  (0.0, 20.0, 2.0),
  (20.0, 40.0, 3.0),
  (40.0, 60.0, 4.0),
  (60.0, 100.0, 5.0)
]
ndvi_class = service.getProcess(
  "Coverage.reclass"
).execute(ndvi_clip, ndvi_rules, NaN_value)

ndvi_min = -100.0
ndvi_max = 100.0

geo_res_dem = service.getProcess(
  "Coverage.geoDetector"
).execute(
  ndvi_clip,
  "elevation",
  dem_class,
  ndvi_min,
  ndvi_max,
  NaN_value
)

geo_res_slope = service.getProcess(
  "Coverage.geoDetector"
).execute(
  ndvi_clip,
  "slope",
  slope_class,
  ndvi_min,
  ndvi_max,
  NaN_value
)

geo_res_aspect = service.getProcess(
  "Coverage.geoDetector"
).execute(
  ndvi_clip,
  "aspect",
  aspect_class,
  ndvi_min,
  ndvi_max,
  NaN_value
)

geo_res_relief = service.getProcess(
  "Coverage.geoDetector"
).execute(
  ndvi_clip,
  "relief",
  relief_class,
  ndvi_min,
  ndvi_max,
  NaN_value
)

ndvi_vis = {
  "min": 1,
  "max": 5,
  "palette": [
    "#f7fcf5",
    "#c7e9c0",
    "#74c476",
    "#31a354",
    "#006d2c"
  ]
}

ndvi_class.styles(ndvi_vis).getMap("vegetationdistributionresult")

geo_res_dem.log("elevation factor explanation result")
geo_res_slope.log("slopefactor result")
geo_res_aspect.log("aspectfactor result")
geo_res_relief.log("local relief factor explanation result")

oge.mapclient.centerMap(108.43, 33.17, 10)

name: Coverage.mod
display_name: replace pixel values with another image
functional_semantic: Replaces or modifies paired raster pixel values using values from another coverage according to band-pairing rules.
details_description: Pairs bands in coverage1 and coverage2: if either image has only one band, that single band is paired with all bands of the other image; if both have the same number of bands but different names, bands are paired in natural order. For each pair, pixel values in coverage1 are modified to the values from coverage2. Output band naming follows the input with more bands, or coverage1 when band counts are equal. The output pixel type is the union of the two input types.

name: Coverage.reclass
functional_semantic: Reclassifies raster values according to mapping rules and NoData settings.
details_description: Applies reclassification rules to an input Coverage in TIF form, such as mapping value ranges to new values, and can specify NaN_value handling for invalid input values. It outputs the reclassified Coverage in TIF form.

examples.title: Example: reclassification
examples.description: Load a DEM raster, define interval rules, run reclassification, then set a palette for visualization.
examples.code:
import oge

# ========== Initialize ==========
oge.initialize()
service = oge.Service()

# ========== 1. raster data（ DEM ）==========
dem = service.getCoverage(coverageID="ASTGTM_N43E084", productID="ASTER_GDEM_DEM30")

# ========== 2. reclassification ==========
# format (min_value, max_value, new_value)
# ：elevation 0–2000m 1，2000–3000m2，3000–4500m3，>4500m4
rules = [
  (0.0, 2000.0, 1.0),
  (2000.0, 3000.0, 2.0),
  (3000.0, 4500.0, 3.0),
  (4500.0, 9999.0, 4.0)
]

# ========== 3. reclassification ==========
# NaN_value ， -9999 or 0
reclass_result = service.getProcess("Coverage.reclass").execute(dem, rules, 0)

# ========== 4. Visualization ==========
vis_params = {
  "min": 1.0,
  "max": 4.0,
  "palette": ["yellow", "green", "red", "blue"]
}

# ========== 5. displayresult ==========
reclass_result.styles(vis_params).getMap("DEM reclassificationresult")
oge.mapclient.centerMap(84, 43, 8)

import oge

name: FeatureCollection.merge
functional_semantic: Merges two feature collections or layers.
details_description: Merges two input feature collections or layer datasets into one new feature collection and outputs it.

examples.title: Example: merge layers or feature collections
examples.description: Construct two feature collections separately, merge them, and output the merged feature collection.
examples.code:
import oge

# 1. Initialize
oge.initialize()
service = oge.Service()

# 2. geometric
coords1 = [[[35.0, 10.0], [35.0, 15.0], [40.0, 15.0], [40.0, 10.0], [35.0, 10.0]]]
geometry1 = service.getProcess("Geometry.Polygon").execute(
  coordinates=coords1, 
  crs="EPSG:4326"
)

coords2 = [[[36.0, 11.0], [36.0, 14.0], [39.0, 14.0], [39.0, 11.0], [36.0, 11.0]]]
geometry2 = service.getProcess("Geometry.Polygon").execute(
  coordinates=coords2, 
  crs="EPSG:4326"
)

# 3. 
featureCollection1 = service.getProcess("FeatureCollection.loadFromGeometry").execute(geometry1, "{a:10}")
featureCollection2 = service.getProcess("FeatureCollection.loadFromGeometry").execute(geometry2, "{a:15}")

# 4. merge
result = service.getProcess("FeatureCollection.merge").execute(featureCollection1, featureCollection2)


# 5. Set style and display on map
result.styles(["#FF0000"]).getMap("boundary")
oge.mapclient.centerMap(36.5, 14.0, 5)

name: Kernel.chebyshev
functional_semantic: Generates a Chebyshev distance kernel for convolution or focal raster operations.
details_description: Generates a kernel based on Chebyshev distance with the given radius. Kernel values can be normalized or scaled by a given magnitude. The example generates the kernel and uses it to convolve an image.

examples.title: Example: Chebyshev distance kernel
examples.description: Select a single band from an image, generate a Chebyshev distance kernel, and perform convolution.
examples.code:
import oge

oge.initialize()
service = oge.Service()

ls8 = service.getCoverage(coverageID="LC81220392015275LGN00", productID="LC08_L1T")
b3 = service.getProcess("Coverage.selectBands").execute(ls8, ["B3"])

details_description: Approximates the second derivative of the image function discretely. It is sensitive to regions with rapid pixel-intensity changes such as edges and corners and has zero response in uniform regions. The “4” indicates the discrete form using the four neighbours above, below, left, and right of the center pixel. It can be used with Coverage.convolve to convolve rasters.

raster.styles(vis_params).getMap("valuePrediction")

name: SpatialStats.SpatialRegression.SpatialLagModel.fit
functional_semantic: Fits a Spatial Lag Model using vector attributes.
details_description: Takes vector feature data and specifies dependent and independent variable attributes, builds a spatial lag regression model, and outputs feature data containing fitted results. It is suitable for analyzing dependencies and regression effects among spatially adjacent units.

inputs.name: featureRDD
inputs.description: Input vector feature data or feature collection.

examples.title: Example: spatial lag model
examples.description: Load vector data, fit a Spatial Lag Model, and rasterize the result field for display.
examples.code:
import oge
oge.initialize()
service = oge.Service.initialize()
# use your data for getFeature
feature= service.getProcess("Feature_deprecated.load").execute("EasternChina_PopulationAging_Vector","null","EPSG:4326")
feature.styles(["#000000"]).getMap("Aging")
oge.mapclient.centerMap(115,31,4)
# SpatialLagModel
result = service.getProcess("SpatialStats.SpatialRegression.SpatialLagModel.fit").execute(feature,"aging","PCGDP,GI,FD,education") 
raster = service.getProcess("Feature_deprecated.rasterizeByGDAL").execute(result,"fitValue",0,"False","1",0.01,0.01,"",0)
vis_params = {"palette": ["#ffffcc", "#ffeda0", "#fed976", "#feb24c", "#fd8d3c","#fc4e2a","#e31a1c","#bd0026","#800026"]}
raster.styles(vis_params).getMap("fitValue")
